1	from dataclasses import dataclass
2	from pathlib import Path
3	from textwrap import dedent
4	
5	
6	@dataclass(frozen=True)
7	class Change:
8	    title: str
9	    desc: str | None
10	    shoulds: list[str]
11	    musts: list[str]
12	    approvals: list[str]
13	    branch: str | None
14	    log_path: Path
15	
16	    def _format(self, title: str, steps: list[str]) -> str:
17	        if len(steps) == 0:
18	            return ""
19	        return title + "\n" + "\n".join(f"- {step}" for step in steps)
20	
21	    def full_description(self) -> str:
22	        return dedent(
23	            f"""
24	            --- {self.title} ---
25	            {self.desc}
26	
27	            {self._format("You SHOULD:", self.shoulds)}
28	
29	            {self._format("You MUST:", self.musts)}
30	            """
31	        ).strip()
32	
33	
34	class ChangeBuilder:
35	    def __init__(self, title: str):
36	        self._title = title
37	        self._desc = None
38	        self._shoulds: list[str] = []
39	        self._musts: list[str] = []
40	        self._approvals: list[str] = []
41	        self._branch = None
42	        self._log_path = None
43	
44	    def desc(self, desc: str) -> "ChangeBuilder":
45	        self._desc = dedent(desc).strip()
46	        return self
47	
48	    def shoulds(self, items: list[str]) -> "ChangeBuilder":
49	        self._shoulds = list(items)
50	        return self
51	
52	    def musts(self, items: list[str]) -> "ChangeBuilder":
53	        self._musts = list(items)
54	        return self
55	
56	    def approvals(self, items: list[str]) -> "ChangeBuilder":
57	        self._approvals = list(items)
58	        return self
59	
60	    def branch(self, branch: str) -> "ChangeBuilder":
61	        self._branch = branch
62	        return self
63	
64	    def log_path(self, log_path: Path) -> "ChangeBuilder":
65	        self._log_path = log_path
66	        return self
67	
68	    def build(self) -> Change:
69	        return Change(
70	            title=self._title,
71	            desc=self._desc,
72	            shoulds=self._shoulds,
73	            musts=self._musts,
74	            approvals=self._approvals,
75	            branch=self._branch,
76	            log_path=self._log_path,
77	        )

1	import pytest
2	from pathlib import Path
3	from tim.project import MacSandboxProject
4	import uuid
5	
6	
7	@pytest.fixture
8	def project(tmp_path):
9	    return MacSandboxProject(root=tmp_path)
10	
11	
12	def test_run_returns_stdout(project):
13	    result = project.run("echo hello", timeout=5)
14	    assert result.returncode == 0
15	    assert result.stdout.strip() == "hello"
16	
17	
18	def test_run_returns_nonzero_exit(project):
19	    result = project.run("sh -c 'exit 42'", timeout=5)
20	    assert result.returncode == 42
21	
22	
23	def test_run_write_inside_root_allowed(project):
24	    target = project.root / "output.txt"
25	    result = project.run(f"sh -c 'echo ok > {target}'", timeout=5)
26	    assert result.returncode == 0
27	    assert target.read_text().strip() == "ok"
28	
29	
30	def test_run_write_outside_root_denied(project):
31	    # /private/var/tmp is world-writable on macOS but is NOT in the sandbox allowlist
32	    outside = Path(f"/private/var/tmp/sandbox_escape_test_{uuid.uuid4().hex}.txt")
33	    outside.unlink(missing_ok=True)
34	    try:
35	        project.run(f"sh -c 'echo bad > {outside}'", timeout=5)
36	        assert not outside.exists(), "sandbox should have blocked the write"
37	    finally:
38	        outside.unlink(missing_ok=True)
39	
40	
41	def test_run_timeout(project):
42	    result = project.run("sleep 60", timeout=1)
43	    assert result.returncode == -1
44	    assert "timeout" in result.stdout.lower()

import pytest
from pathlib import Path
from dataclasses import FrozenInstanceError
from tim.change import Change, ChangeBuilder


# ============================================================
# Change dataclass tests
# ============================================================


class TestChangeDataclass:
    """Tests for the Change dataclass."""

    def test_change_is_frozen(self):
        """Test that Change is immutable (frozen)."""
        change = Change(
            title="Test Title",
            desc="Test description",
            shoulds=["should1"],
            musts=["must1"],
            approvals=["approver1"],
            branch="feature/test",
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        # Attempting to modify any field should raise FrozenInstanceError
        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.title = "Modified Title"

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.desc = "Modified description"

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.shoulds = ["modified"]

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.musts = ["modified"]

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.approvals = ["modified"]

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.branch = "modified"

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.log_path = Path("/modified/path")

    def test_change_field_access(self):
        """Test that all fields can be accessed."""
        change = Change(
            title="My Change",
            desc="A description",
            shoulds=["should do this"],
            musts=["must do that"],
            approvals=["alice", "bob"],
            branch="feature/my-feature",
            log_path=Path("/var/log/change.log"),
        )

        assert change.title == "My Change"
        assert change.desc == "A description"
        assert change.shoulds == ["should do this"]
        assert change.musts == ["must do that"]
        assert change.approvals == ["alice", "bob"]
        assert change.branch == "feature/my-feature"
        assert change.log_path == Path("/var/log/change.log")

    def test_change_with_none_desc(self):
        """Test Change with None description."""
        change = Change(
            title="No Description",
            desc=None,
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        assert change.title == "No Description"
        assert change.desc is None

    def test_change_with_all_empty_lists(self):
        """Test Change with all empty lists."""
        change = Change(
            title="Empty Lists",
            desc="Some description",
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        assert change.shoulds == []
        assert change.musts == []
        assert change.approvals == []

    def test_change_with_none_branch(self):
        """Test Change with None branch."""
        change = Change(
            title="No Branch",
            desc=None,
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        assert change.branch is None

    def test_change_equality(self):
        """Test that two Change instances with same fields are equal."""
        change1 = Change(
            title="Same Title",
            desc="Same desc",
            shoulds=["s1"],
            musts=["m1"],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )
        change2 = Change(
            title="Same Title",
            desc="Same desc",
            shoulds=["s1"],
            musts=["m1"],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        assert change1 == change2

    def test_change_inequality(self):
        """Test that two Change instances with different fields are not equal."""
        change1 = Change(
            title="Title1",
            desc="Desc1",
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )
        change2 = Change(
            title="Title2",
            desc="Desc1",
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        assert change1 != change2


# ============================================================
# Change._format() tests
# ============================================================


class TestChangeFormat:
    """Tests for Change._format() method."""

    def _make_change(self, **kwargs):
        """Helper to create a minimal Change for testing _format."""
        return Change(
            title="Test",
            desc=None,
            shoulds=kwargs.get("shoulds", []),
            musts=kwargs.get("musts", []),
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

    def test_format_empty_steps(self):
        """Test _format with empty steps returns empty string."""
        change = self._make_change()
        assert change._format("Test Title", []) == ""

    def test_format_single_step(self):
        """Test _format with a single step."""
        change = self._make_change()
        result = change._format("Steps:", ["first step"])
        assert result == "Steps:\n- first step"

    def test_format_multiple_steps(self):
        """Test _format with multiple steps."""
        change = self._make_change()
        result = change._format("Steps:", ["step 1", "step 2", "step 3"])
        assert result == "Steps:\n- step 1\n- step 2\n- step 3"

    def test_format_step_with_special_chars(self):
        """Test _format preserves special characters in steps."""
        change = self._make_change()
        result = change._format("Notes:", ["do 'this'", 'do "that"', "do & stuff"])
        assert result == "Notes:\n- do 'this'\n- do \"that\"\n- do & stuff"

    def test_format_step_with_newline_in_step(self):
        """Test _format handles steps that might contain newlines."""
        change = self._make_change()
        result = change._format("Steps:", ["step with\nembedded newline"])
        assert result == "Steps:\n- step with\nembedded newline"


# ============================================================
# Change.full_description() tests
# ============================================================


class TestChangeFullDescription:
    """Tests for Change.full_description() method."""

    def test_basic_title_only(self):
        """Test full_description with just a title."""
        change = Change(
            title="My Change",
            desc=None,
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert result == "--- My Change ---"

    def test_with_description(self):
        """Test full_description with a description."""
        change = Change(
            title="Add Feature",
            desc="This adds a new feature",
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert "--- Add Feature ---" in result
        assert "This adds a new feature" in result

    def test_with_shoulds(self):
        """Test full_description with shoulds."""
        change = Change(
            title="Implement Login",
            desc="Add user authentication",
            shoulds=["Handle invalid credentials", "Show error message"],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert "You SHOULD:" in result
        assert "- Handle invalid credentials" in result
        assert "- Show error message" in result

    def test_with_musts(self):
        """Test full_description with musts."""
        change = Change(
            title="Fix Security Bug",
            desc="Patch SQL injection vulnerability",
            shoulds=[],
            musts=["Use parameterized queries", "Validate all inputs"],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert "You MUST:" in result
        assert "- Use parameterized queries" in result
        assert "- Validate all inputs" in result

    def test_with_both_shoulds_and_musts(self):
        """Test full_description with both shoulds and musts."""
        change = Change(
            title="Refactor Module",
            desc="Improve code structure",
            shoulds=["Add logging", "Update docstrings"],
            musts=["Pass all existing tests", "Maintain API compatibility"],
            approvals=["senior-dev"],
            branch="refactor/module",
            log_path=Path("/var/log/refactor.log"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert "--- Refactor Module ---" in result
        assert "Improve code structure" in result
        assert "You SHOULD:" in result
        assert "Add logging" in result
        assert "Update docstrings" in result
        assert "You MUST:" in result
        assert "Pass all existing tests" in result
        assert "Maintain API compatibility" in result

    def test_with_none_description(self):
        """Test full_description with None description."""
        change = Change(
            title="Quick Fix",
            desc=None,
            shoulds=["Check edge cases"],
            musts=["Fix the bug"],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert "--- Quick Fix ---" in result
        assert "You SHOULD:" in result
        assert "Check edge cases" in result
        assert "You MUST:" in result
        assert "Fix the bug" in result
        # When desc is None, the None should not appear in output
        assert "None" not in result

    def test_with_empty_lists(self):
        """Test full_description with empty shoulds/musts lists."""
        change = Change(
            title="Simple Task",
            desc="A simple task with no requirements",
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert "--- Simple Task ---" in result
        assert "A simple task with no requirements" in result
        # Empty shoulds/musts should not appear (formatted to empty string)
        assert "You SHOULD:" not in result
        assert "You MUST:" not in result

    def test_output_is_stripped(self):
        """Test that full_description output is stripped (no leading/trailing whitespace)."""
        change = Change(
            title="Test",
            desc=None,
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert result == result.strip()

    def test_full_description_contains_title_separator(self):
        """Test that title is wrapped with --- separators."""
        change = Change(
            title="Important Change",
            desc=None,
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert "--- Important Change ---" in result

    def test_full_description_multiline_description(self):
        """Test full_description with a multi-line description."""
        change = Change(
            title="Complex Change",
            desc="Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3",
            shoulds=["step 1"],
            musts=["step 2"],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert "Line 1" in result
        assert "Line 2" in result
        assert "Line 3" in result
        assert "step 1" in result
        assert "step 2" in result


# ============================================================
# ChangeBuilder tests
# ============================================================


class TestChangeBuilder:
    """Tests for ChangeBuilder class."""

    def test_basic_build_with_just_title(self):
        """Test building a Change with only a title."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("My Change").build()

        assert change.title == "My Change"
        assert change.desc is None
        assert change.shoulds == []
        assert change.musts == []
        assert change.approvals == []
        assert change.branch is None
        assert change.log_path is None

    def test_building_with_all_fields(self):
        """Test building a Change with all fields set."""
        change = (
            ChangeBuilder("Complete Change")
            .desc("This is a complete change description")
            .shoulds(["should1", "should2"])
            .musts(["must1", "must2"])
            .approvals(["alice", "bob"])
            .branch("feature/complete")
            .log_path(Path("/var/log/complete.log"))
            .build()
        )

        assert change.title == "Complete Change"
        assert change.desc == "This is a complete change description"
        assert change.shoulds == ["should1", "should2"]
        assert change.musts == ["must1", "must2"]
        assert change.approvals == ["alice", "bob"]
        assert change.branch == "feature/complete"
        assert change.log_path == Path("/var/log/complete.log")

    def test_chaining_methods(self):
        """Test that builder methods can be chained."""
        # Verify that each method returns self for chaining
        builder = ChangeBuilder("Chained")
        assert builder.desc("desc").shoulds(["s1"]) is not None
        assert builder.musts(["m1"]).approvals(["a1"]) is not None
        assert builder.branch("main").log_path(Path("/tmp")) is not None

        change = builder.build()
        assert change.title == "Chained"
        assert change.desc == "desc"
        assert change.shoulds == ["s1"]
        assert change.musts == ["m1"]
        assert change.approvals == ["a1"]
        assert change.branch == "main"
        assert change.log_path == Path("/tmp")

    def test_default_values_for_optional_fields(self):
        """Test that optional fields have correct default values."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").build()

        # Verify default values
        assert change.desc is None
        assert change.shoulds == []
        assert change.musts == []
        assert change.approvals == []
        assert change.branch is None
        assert change.log_path is None

    def test_desc_method_with_dedent(self):
        """Test that desc() applies dedent and strip."""
        change = (
            ChangeBuilder("Test")
            .desc(
                """
                This is a multi-line description.
                It has leading indentation.
                """
            )
            .build()
        )

        # dedent should remove common leading whitespace
        assert change.desc == "This is a multi-line description.\nIt has leading indentation."

    def test_desc_method_strips_whitespace(self):
        """Test that desc() strips leading/trailing whitespace."""
        change = (
            ChangeBuilder("Test")
            .desc("   \n  leading and trailing spaces  \n  ")
            .build()
        )

        assert change.desc == "leading and trailing spaces"

    def test_shoulds_with_empty_list(self):
        """Test shoulds() with an empty list."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").shoulds([]).build()
        assert change.shoulds == []

    def test_shoulds_with_items(self):
        """Test shoulds() with items."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").shoulds(["item1", "item2"]).build()
        assert change.shoulds == ["item1", "item2"]

    def test_shoulds_copies_list(self):
        """Test that shoulds() creates a copy (doesn't share reference)."""
        items = ["item1"]
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").shoulds(items).build()

        # Modifying original list shouldn't affect the Change
        items.append("item2")
        assert change.shoulds == ["item1"]

    def test_musts_with_empty_list(self):
        """Test musts() with an empty list."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").musts([]).build()
        assert change.musts == []

    def test_musts_with_items(self):
        """Test musts() with items."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").musts(["item1", "item2"]).build()
        assert change.musts == ["item1", "item2"]

    def test_musts_copies_list(self):
        """Test that musts() creates a copy (doesn't share reference)."""
        items = ["item1"]
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").musts(items).build()

        # Modifying original list shouldn't affect the Change
        items.append("item2")
        assert change.musts == ["item1"]

    def test_approvals_with_empty_list(self):
        """Test approvals() with an empty list."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").approvals([]).build()
        assert change.approvals == []

    def test_approvals_with_items(self):
        """Test approvals() with items."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").approvals(["alice", "bob"]).build()
        assert change.approvals == ["alice", "bob"]

    def test_approvals_copies_list(self):
        """Test that approvals() creates a copy (doesn't share reference)."""
        items = ["alice"]
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").approvals(items).build()

        # Modifying original list shouldn't affect the Change
        items.append("bob")
        assert change.approvals == ["alice"]

    def test_branch_setting_branch_name(self):
        """Test branch() setting the branch name."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").branch("feature/test-branch").build()
        assert change.branch == "feature/test-branch"

    def test_branch_default_is_none(self):
        """Test that branch defaults to None."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").build()
        assert change.branch is None

    def test_log_path_setting_log_path(self):
        """Test log_path() setting the log path."""
        path = Path("/var/log/my-change.log")
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").log_path(path).build()
        assert change.log_path == path

    def test_log_path_default_is_none(self):
        """Test that log_path defaults to None."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").build()
        assert change.log_path is None

    def test_builder_returns_change_instance(self):
        """Test that build() returns a Change instance."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").build()
        assert isinstance(change, Change)

    def test_multiple_builds_independent(self):
        """Test that building multiple Changes from same builder pattern works."""
        change1 = ChangeBuilder("First").desc("First desc").build()
        change2 = ChangeBuilder("Second").desc("Second desc").build()

        assert change1.title == "First"
        assert change2.title == "Second"
        assert change1.desc == "First desc"
        assert change2.desc == "Second desc"

    def test_desc_with_complex_indentation(self):
        """Test desc() with complex indentation patterns."""
        change = (
            ChangeBuilder("Test")
            .desc(
                """
                    This has 4 spaces of indentation.
                        And this has 8 spaces of indentation.
                    Back to 4 spaces.
                """
            )
            .build()
        )

        # dedent should remove the common 4-space indentation
        assert "This has 4 spaces of indentation." in change.desc
        assert "And this has 8 spaces of indentation." in change.desc
        assert "Back to 4 spaces." in change.desc

    def test_full_builder_workflow(self):
        """Test a complete builder workflow similar to actual usage."""
        change = (
            ChangeBuilder("Add user authentication")
            .desc(
                """
                Implement OAuth2 authentication for the API.
                This will allow third-party applications to
                access the API securely.
                """
            )
            .shoulds([
                "Use PKCE for public clients",
                "Refresh tokens after expiration",
                "Cache access tokens efficiently",
            ])
            .musts([
                "Pass all security audit checks",
                "Support RFC 6749 compliance",
                "Log all authentication attempts",
            ])
            .approvals(["security-lead", "architect"])
            .branch("feature/oauth2-auth")
            .log_path(Path("/var/log/auth-feature.log"))
            .build()
        )

        assert change.title == "Add user authentication"
        assert change.branch == "feature/oauth2-auth"
        assert change.log_path == Path("/var/log/auth-feature.log")
        assert len(change.shoulds) == 3
        assert len(change.musts) == 3
        assert len(change.approvals) == 2

import pytest
from pathlib import Path
from dataclasses import FrozenInstanceError
from tim.change import Change, ChangeBuilder


# ============================================================
# Change dataclass tests
# ============================================================


class TestChangeDataclass:
    """Tests for the Change dataclass."""

    def test_change_is_frozen(self):
        """Test that Change is immutable (frozen)."""
        change = Change(
            title="Test Title",
            desc="Test description",
            shoulds=["should1"],
            musts=["must1"],
            approvals=["approver1"],
            branch="feature/test",
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        # Attempting to modify any field should raise FrozenInstanceError
        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.title = "Modified Title"

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.desc = "Modified description"

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.shoulds = ["modified"]

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.musts = ["modified"]

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.approvals = ["modified"]

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.branch = "modified"

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.log_path = Path("/modified/path")

    def test_change_field_access(self):
        """Test that all fields can be accessed."""
        change = Change(
            title="My Change",
            desc="A description",
            shoulds=["should do this"],
            musts=["must do that"],
            approvals=["alice", "bob"],
            branch="feature/my-feature",
            log_path=Path("/var/log/change.log"),
        )

        assert change.title == "My Change"
        assert change.desc == "A description"
        assert change.shoulds == ["should do this"]
        assert change.musts == ["must do that"]
        assert change.approvals == ["alice", "bob"]
        assert change.branch == "feature/my-feature"
        assert change.log_path == Path("/var/log/change.log")

    def test_change_with_none_desc(self):
        """Test Change with None description."""
        change = Change(
            title="No Description",
            desc=None,
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        assert change.title == "No Description"
        assert change.desc is None

    def test_change_with_all_empty_lists(self):
        """Test Change with all empty lists."""
        change = Change(
            title="Empty Lists",
            desc="Some description",
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        assert change.shoulds == []
        assert change.musts == []
        assert change.approvals == []

    def test_change_with_none_branch(self):
        """Test Change with None branch."""
        change = Change(
            title="No Branch",
            desc=None,
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        assert change.branch is None

    def test_change_equality(self):
        """Test that two Change instances with same fields are equal."""
        change1 = Change(
            title="Same Title",
            desc="Same desc",
            shoulds=["s1"],
            musts=["m1"],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )
        change2 = Change(
            title="Same Title",
            desc="Same desc",
            shoulds=["s1"],
            musts=["m1"],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        assert change1 == change2

    def test_change_inequality(self):
        """Test that two Change instances with different fields are not equal."""
        change1 = Change(
            title="Title1",
            desc="Desc1",
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )
        change2 = Change(
            title="Title2",
            desc="Desc1",
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        assert change1 != change2


# ============================================================
# Change._format() tests
# ============================================================


class TestChangeFormat:
    """Tests for Change._format() method."""

    def _make_change(self, **kwargs):
        """Helper to create a minimal Change for testing _format."""
        return Change(
            title="Test",
            desc=None,
            shoulds=kwargs.get("shoulds", []),
            musts=kwargs.get("musts", []),
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

    def test_format_empty_steps(self):
        """Test _format with empty steps returns empty string."""
        change = self._make_change()
        assert change._format("Test Title", []) == ""

    def test_format_single_step(self):
        """Test _format with a single step."""
        change = self._make_change()
        result = change._format("Steps:", ["first step"])
        assert result == "Steps:\n- first step"

    def test_format_multiple_steps(self):
        """Test _format with multiple steps."""
        change = self._make_change()
        result = change._format("Steps:", ["step 1", "step 2", "step 3"])
        assert result == "Steps:\n- step 1\n- step 2\n- step 3"

    def test_format_step_with_special_chars(self):
        """Test _format preserves special characters in steps."""
        change = self._make_change()
        result = change._format("Notes:", ["do 'this'", 'do "that"', "do & stuff"])
        assert result == "Notes:\n- do 'this'\n- do \"that\"\n- do & stuff"

    def test_format_step_with_newline_in_step(self):
        """Test _format handles steps that might contain newlines."""
        change = self._make_change()
        result = change._format("Steps:", ["step with\nembedded newline"])
        assert result == "Steps:\n- step with\nembedded newline"


# ============================================================
# Change.full_description() tests
# ============================================================


class TestChangeFullDescription:
    """Tests for Change.full_description() method."""

    def test_basic_title_only(self):
        """Test full_description with just a title."""
        change = Change(
            title="My Change",
            desc=None,
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        # When desc is None, it becomes the string "None" in the f-string
        assert "--- My Change ---" in result
        assert "None" in result  # None gets converted to string "None"

    def test_with_description(self):
        """Test full_description with a description."""
        change = Change(
            title="Add Feature",
            desc="This adds a new feature",
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert "--- Add Feature ---" in result
        assert "This adds a new feature" in result
        assert "None" not in result  # desc is a real string, not None

    def test_with_shoulds(self):
        """Test full_description with shoulds."""
        change = Change(
            title="Implement Login",
            desc="Add user authentication",
            shoulds=["Handle invalid credentials", "Show error message"],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert "You SHOULD:" in result
        assert "- Handle invalid credentials" in result
        assert "- Show error message" in result

    def test_with_musts(self):
        """Test full_description with musts."""
        change = Change(
            title="Fix Security Bug",
            desc="Patch SQL injection vulnerability",
            shoulds=[],
            musts=["Use parameterized queries", "Validate all inputs"],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert "You MUST:" in result
        assert "- Use parameterized queries" in result
        assert "- Validate all inputs" in result

    def test_with_both_shoulds_and_musts(self):
        """Test full_description with both shoulds and musts."""
        change = Change(
            title="Refactor Module",
            desc="Improve code structure",
            shoulds=["Add logging", "Update docstrings"],
            musts=["Pass all existing tests", "Maintain API compatibility"],
            approvals=["senior-dev"],
            branch="refactor/module",
            log_path=Path("/var/log/refactor.log"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert "--- Refactor Module ---" in result
        assert "Improve code structure" in result
        assert "You SHOULD:" in result
        assert "Add logging" in result
        assert "Update docstrings" in result
        assert "You MUST:" in result
        assert "Pass all existing tests" in result
        assert "Maintain API compatibility" in result

    def test_with_none_description(self):
        """Test full_description with None description - None appears as string."""
        change = Change(
            title="Quick Fix",
            desc=None,
            shoulds=["Check edge cases"],
            musts=["Fix the bug"],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert "--- Quick Fix ---" in result
        assert "You SHOULD:" in result
        assert "Check edge cases" in result
        assert "You MUST:" in result
        assert "Fix the bug" in result
        # When desc is None, it becomes the string "None" in the output
        assert "None" in result

    def test_with_empty_lists(self):
        """Test full_description with empty shoulds/musts lists."""
        change = Change(
            title="Simple Task",
            desc="A simple task with no requirements",
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert "--- Simple Task ---" in result
        assert "A simple task with no requirements" in result
        # Empty shoulds/musts should not appear (formatted to empty string)
        assert "You SHOULD:" not in result
        assert "You MUST:" not in result

    def test_output_is_stripped(self):
        """Test that full_description output is stripped (no leading/trailing whitespace)."""
        change = Change(
            title="Test",
            desc=None,
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert result == result.strip()

    def test_full_description_contains_title_separator(self):
        """Test that title is wrapped with --- separators."""
        change = Change(
            title="Important Change",
            desc=None,
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert "--- Important Change ---" in result

    def test_full_description_multiline_description(self):
        """Test full_description with a multi-line description."""
        change = Change(
            title="Complex Change",
            desc="Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3",
            shoulds=["step 1"],
            musts=["step 2"],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert "Line 1" in result
        assert "Line 2" in result
        assert "Line 3" in result
        assert "step 1" in result
        assert "step 2" in result


# ============================================================
# ChangeBuilder tests
# ============================================================


class TestChangeBuilder:
    """Tests for ChangeBuilder class."""

    def test_basic_build_with_just_title(self):
        """Test building a Change with only a title."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("My Change").build()

        assert change.title == "My Change"
        assert change.desc is None
        assert change.shoulds == []
        assert change.musts == []
        assert change.approvals == []
        assert change.branch is None
        assert change.log_path is None

    def test_building_with_all_fields(self):
        """Test building a Change with all fields set."""
        change = (
            ChangeBuilder("Complete Change")
            .desc("This is a complete change description")
            .shoulds(["should1", "should2"])
            .musts(["must1", "must2"])
            .approvals(["alice", "bob"])
            .branch("feature/complete")
            .log_path(Path("/var/log/complete.log"))
            .build()
        )

        assert change.title == "Complete Change"
        assert change.desc == "This is a complete change description"
        assert change.shoulds == ["should1", "should2"]
        assert change.musts == ["must1", "must2"]
        assert change.approvals == ["alice", "bob"]
        assert change.branch == "feature/complete"
        assert change.log_path == Path("/var/log/complete.log")

    def test_chaining_methods(self):
        """Test that builder methods can be chained."""
        # Verify that each method returns self for chaining
        builder = ChangeBuilder("Chained")
        assert builder.desc("desc").shoulds(["s1"]) is not None
        assert builder.musts(["m1"]).approvals(["a1"]) is not None
        assert builder.branch("main").log_path(Path("/tmp")) is not None

        change = builder.build()
        assert change.title == "Chained"
        assert change.desc == "desc"
        assert change.shoulds == ["s1"]
        assert change.musts == ["m1"]
        assert change.approvals == ["a1"]
        assert change.branch == "main"
        assert change.log_path == Path("/tmp")

    def test_default_values_for_optional_fields(self):
        """Test that optional fields have correct default values."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").build()

        # Verify default values
        assert change.desc is None
        assert change.shoulds == []
        assert change.musts == []
        assert change.approvals == []
        assert change.branch is None
        assert change.log_path is None

    def test_desc_method_with_dedent(self):
        """Test that desc() applies dedent and strip."""
        change = (
            ChangeBuilder("Test")
            .desc(
                """
                This is a multi-line description.
                It has leading indentation.
                """
            )
            .build()
        )

        # dedent should remove common leading whitespace
        assert change.desc == "This is a multi-line description.\nIt has leading indentation."

    def test_desc_method_strips_whitespace(self):
        """Test that desc() strips leading/trailing whitespace."""
        change = (
            ChangeBuilder("Test")
            .desc("   \n  leading and trailing spaces  \n  ")
            .build()
        )

        assert change.desc == "leading and trailing spaces"

    def test_shoulds_with_empty_list(self):
        """Test shoulds() with an empty list."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").shoulds([]).build()
        assert change.shoulds == []

    def test_shoulds_with_items(self):
        """Test shoulds() with items."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").shoulds(["item1", "item2"]).build()
        assert change.shoulds == ["item1", "item2"]

    def test_shoulds_copies_list(self):
        """Test that shoulds() creates a copy (doesn't share reference)."""
        items = ["item1"]
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").shoulds(items).build()

        # Modifying original list shouldn't affect the Change
        items.append("item2")
        assert change.shoulds == ["item1"]

    def test_musts_with_empty_list(self):
        """Test musts() with an empty list."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").musts([]).build()
        assert change.musts == []

    def test_musts_with_items(self):
        """Test musts() with items."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").musts(["item1", "item2"]).build()
        assert change.musts == ["item1", "item2"]

    def test_musts_copies_list(self):
        """Test that musts() creates a copy (doesn't share reference)."""
        items = ["item1"]
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").musts(items).build()

        # Modifying original list shouldn't affect the Change
        items.append("item2")
        assert change.musts == ["item1"]

    def test_approvals_with_empty_list(self):
        """Test approvals() with an empty list."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").approvals([]).build()
        assert change.approvals == []

    def test_approvals_with_items(self):
        """Test approvals() with items."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").approvals(["alice", "bob"]).build()
        assert change.approvals == ["alice", "bob"]

    def test_approvals_copies_list(self):
        """Test that approvals() creates a copy (doesn't share reference)."""
        items = ["alice"]
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").approvals(items).build()

        # Modifying original list shouldn't affect the Change
        items.append("bob")
        assert change.approvals == ["alice"]

    def test_branch_setting_branch_name(self):
        """Test branch() setting the branch name."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").branch("feature/test-branch").build()
        assert change.branch == "feature/test-branch"

    def test_branch_default_is_none(self):
        """Test that branch defaults to None."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").build()
        assert change.branch is None

    def test_log_path_setting_log_path(self):
        """Test log_path() setting the log path."""
        path = Path("/var/log/my-change.log")
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").log_path(path).build()
        assert change.log_path == path

    def test_log_path_default_is_none(self):
        """Test that log_path defaults to None."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").build()
        assert change.log_path is None

    def test_builder_returns_change_instance(self):
        """Test that build() returns a Change instance."""
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").build()
        assert isinstance(change, Change)

    def test_multiple_builds_independent(self):
        """Test that building multiple Changes from same builder pattern works."""
        change1 = ChangeBuilder("First").desc("First desc").build()
        change2 = ChangeBuilder("Second").desc("Second desc").build()

        assert change1.title == "First"
        assert change2.title == "Second"
        assert change1.desc == "First desc"
        assert change2.desc == "Second desc"

    def test_desc_with_complex_indentation(self):
        """Test desc() with complex indentation patterns."""
        change = (
            ChangeBuilder("Test")
            .desc(
                """
                    This has 4 spaces of indentation.
                        And this has 8 spaces of indentation.
                    Back to 4 spaces.
                """
            )
            .build()
        )

        # dedent should remove the common 4-space indentation
        assert "This has 4 spaces of indentation." in change.desc
        assert "And this has 8 spaces of indentation." in change.desc
        assert "Back to 4 spaces." in change.desc

    def test_full_builder_workflow(self):
        """Test a complete builder workflow similar to actual usage."""
        change = (
            ChangeBuilder("Add user authentication")
            .desc(
                """
                Implement OAuth2 authentication for the API.
                This will allow third-party applications to
                access the API securely.
                """
            )
            .shoulds([
                "Use PKCE for public clients",
                "Refresh tokens after expiration",
                "Cache access tokens efficiently",
            ])
            .musts([
                "Pass all security audit checks",
                "Support RFC 6749 compliance",
                "Log all authentication attempts",
            ])
            .approvals(["security-lead", "architect"])
            .branch("feature/oauth2-auth")
            .log_path(Path("/var/log/auth-feature.log"))
            .build()
        )

        assert change.title == "Add user authentication"
        assert change.branch == "feature/oauth2-auth"
        assert change.log_path == Path("/var/log/auth-feature.log")
        assert len(change.shoulds) == 3
        assert len(change.musts) == 3
        assert len(change.approvals) == 2

1	from dataclasses import dataclass
2	from pathlib import Path
3	from textwrap import dedent
4	
5	
6	@dataclass(frozen=True)
7	class Change:
8	    title: str
9	    desc: str | None
10	    shoulds: list[str]
11	    musts: list[str]
12	    approvals: list[str]
13	    branch: str | None
14	    log_path: Path
15	
16	    def _format(self, title: str, steps: list[str]) -> str:
17	        if len(steps) == 0:
18	            return ""
19	        return title + "\n" + "\n".join(f"- {step}" for step in steps)
20	
21	    def full_description(self) -> str:
22	        return dedent(
23	            f"""
24	            --- {self.title} ---
25	            {self.desc}
26	
27	            {self._format("You SHOULD:", self.shoulds)}
28	
29	            {self._format("You MUST:", self.musts)}
30	            """
31	        ).strip()
32	
33	
34	class ChangeBuilder:
35	    def __init__(self, title: str):
36	        self._title = title
37	        self._desc = None
38	        self._shoulds: list[str] = []
39	        self._musts: list[str] = []
40	        self._approvals: list[str] = []
41	        self._branch = None
42	        self._log_path = None
43	
44	    def desc(self, desc: str) -> "ChangeBuilder":
45	        self._desc = dedent(desc).strip()
46	        return self
47	
48	    def shoulds(self, items: list[str]) -> "ChangeBuilder":
49	        self._shoulds = list(items)
50	        return self
51	
52	    def musts(self, items: list[str]) -> "ChangeBuilder":
53	        self._musts = list(items)
54	        return self
55	
56	    def approvals(self, items: list[str]) -> "ChangeBuilder":
57	        self._approvals = list(items)
58	        return self
59	
60	    def branch(self, branch: str) -> "ChangeBuilder":
61	        self._branch = branch
62	        return self
63	
64	    def log_path(self, log_path: Path) -> "ChangeBuilder":
65	        self._log_path = log_path
66	        return self
67	
68	    def build(self) -> Change:
69	        return Change(
70	            title=self._title,
71	            desc=self._desc,
72	            shoulds=self._shoulds,
73	            musts=self._musts,
74	            approvals=self._approvals,
75	            branch=self._branch,
76	            log_path=self._log_path,
77	        )

<stdout>from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from textwrap import dedent


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Change:
    title: str
    desc: str | None
    shoulds: list[str]
    musts: list[str]
    approvals: list[str]
    branch: str | None
    log_path: Path

    def _format(self, title: str, steps: list[str]) -> str:
        if len(steps) == 0:
            return ""
        return title + "\n" + "\n".join(f"- {step}" for step in steps)

    def full_description(self) -> str:
        return dedent(
            f"""
            --- {self.title} ---
            {self.desc}

            {self._format("You SHOULD:", self.shoulds)}

            {self._format("You MUST:", self.musts)}

            {self._format("Your change is complete when:", self.approvals)}
            """
        ).strip()


class ChangeBuilder:
    def __init__(self, title: str):
        self._title = title
        self._desc = None
        self._shoulds: list[str] = []
        self._musts: list[str] = []
        self._approvals: list[str] = []
        self._branch = None
        self._log_path = None

    def desc(self, desc: str) -> "ChangeBuilder":
        self._desc = dedent(desc).strip()
        return self

    def shoulds(self, items: list[str]) -> "ChangeBuilder":
        self._shoulds = list(items)
        return self

    def musts(self, items: list[str]) -> "ChangeBuilder":
        self._musts = list(items)
        return self

    def approvals(self, items: list[str]) -> "ChangeBuilder":
        self._approvals = list(items)
        return self

    def branch(self, branch: str) -> "ChangeBuilder":
        self._branch = branch
        return self

    def log_path(self, log_path: Path) -> "ChangeBuilder":
        self._log_path = log_path
        return self

    def build(self) -> Change:
        return Change(
            title=self._title,
            desc=self._desc,
            shoulds=self._shoulds,
            musts=self._musts,
            approvals=self._approvals,
            branch=self._branch,
            log_path=self._log_path,
        )
</stdout>
<stderr></stderr>

9	def _numbered(lines: list[str], offset: int = 1) -> str:
10	    return "".join(f"{offset + i}\t{line}" for i, line in enumerate(lines))
11	
12	
13	def view_file(
14	    project: Project,
15	    path: Annotated[str, "Path to the file to view"],
16	    start_line: Annotated[int | None, "First line to show (1-indexed, inclusive)"] = None,
17	    end_line: Annotated[int | None, "Last line to show (1-indexed, inclusive)"] = None,
18	) -> str:
19	    """Show file contents with line numbers. Optionally restrict to a line range."""
20	    logger.debug(f"view_file {path} lines={start_line}-{end_line}")
21	    try:
22	        lines = project.path(path).read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)
23	    except IsADirectoryError:
24	        return f"Tool Error: {path} is a directory"
25	    except FileNotFoundError:
26	        return f"Tool Error: {path} not found"
27	    start_index = (start_line or 1) - 1
28	    end_index = end_line if end_line is not None else len(lines)
29	    return _numbered(lines[start_index:end_index], offset=start_index + 1)
30	
31	
32	def create_file(
33	    project: Project,
34	    path: Annotated[str, "Path to the new file"],
35	    content: Annotated[str, "File content"],
36	) -> str:
37	    """Create a new file with the given content, creating dirs if required. Overwrites file if already exists."""
38	    logger.debug(f"create_file {path}")
39	    path = project.path(path)
40	    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
41	    if content and not content.endswith("\n"):
42	        content += "\n"
43	    path.write_text(content)
44	    lines = content.splitlines(keepends=True)
45	    return f"Wrote {len(lines)} lines"
46	
47	
48	def edit_file(
49	    project: Project,
50	    path: Annotated[str, "Path to the file to edit"],
51	    start_line: Annotated[int, "First line of the range to replace (1-indexed, inclusive)"],
52	    end_line: Annotated[
53	        int,
54	        "Last line of the range to replace (1-indexed, inclusive). Set to start_line - 1 to insert without replacing.",
55	    ],
56	    new_content: Annotated[str, "Replacement text. Empty string to delete the range."],
57	) -> str:
58	    """Replace, insert, or delete lines in a file. Returns the updated file with line numbers."""
59	    logger.debug(f"edit_file {path} lines={start_line}-{end_line}")
60	    path = project.path(path)
61	    lines = path.read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)
62	    if new_content and not new_content.endswith("\n"):
63	        new_content += "\n"
64	    replacement = new_content.splitlines(keepends=True) if new_content else []
65	    lines[start_line - 1 : end_line] = replacement
66	    result = "".join(lines)
67	    path.write_text(result)
68	    return _numbered(lines)

class TestChangeDataclass:

            shoulds=["should1"],
            musts=["must1"],
            approvals=["approver1"],
            branch="feature/test",
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.title = "Modified Title"

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.desc = "Modified description"

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.shoulds = ["modified"]

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.musts = ["modified"]

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.approvals = ["modified"]

class TestChangeFormat:
    def _make_change(self, **kwargs):
        return Change(
            title="Test",
            desc=None,
            shoulds=kwargs.get("shoulds", []),
            musts=kwargs.get("musts", []),
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

    def test_format_empty_steps(self):
        change = self._make_change()
        assert change._format("Test Title", []) == ""

    def test_format_single_step(self):
        change = self._make_change()
        result = change._format("Steps:", ["first step"])
        assert result == "Steps:\n- first step"

    def test_format_multiple_steps(self):
        change = self._make_change()
        result = change._format("Steps:", ["step 1", "step 2", "step 3"])
        assert result == "Steps:\n- step 1\n- step 2\n- step 3"

    def test_format_step_with_special_chars(self):
        change = self._make_change()
        result = change._format("Notes:", ["do 'this'", 'do "that"', "do & stuff"])
        assert result == "Notes:\n- do 'this'\n- do \"that\"\n- do & stuff"

    def test_format_step_with_newline_in_step(self):
        change = self._make_change()
        result = change._format("Steps:", ["step with\nembedded newline"])
        assert result == "Steps:\n- step with\nembedded newline"

    def test_log_path_default_is_none(self):
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").build()
        assert change.log_path is None

    def test_builder_returns_change_instance(self):
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").build()
        assert isinstance(change, Change)

    def test_multiple_builds_independent(self):
        change1 = ChangeBuilder("First").desc("First desc").build()
        change2 = ChangeBuilder("Second").desc("Second desc").build()

        assert change1.title == "First"
        assert change2.title == "Second"
        assert change1.desc == "First desc"
        assert change2.desc == "Second desc"

    def test_desc_with_complex_indentation(self):
        change = (
            ChangeBuilder("Test")
            .desc(
                """
                    This has 4 spaces of indentation.
                        And this has 8 spaces of indentation.
                    Back to 4 spaces.
                """
            )
            .build()
        )

        assert "This has 4 spaces of indentation." in change.desc
        assert "And this has 8 spaces of indentation." in change.desc
        assert "Back to 4 spaces." in change.desc

    def test_full_builder_workflow(self):
        change = (
            ChangeBuilder("Add user authentication")
            .desc(
                """
                Implement OAuth2 authentication for the API.
                This will allow third-party applications to
                access the API securely.
                """
            )
            .shoulds(
                [
                    "Use PKCE for public clients",
                    "Refresh tokens after expiration",
                    "Cache access tokens efficiently",
                ]
            )
            .musts(
                [
                    "Pass all security audit checks",
                    "Support RFC 6749 compliance",
                    "Log all authentication attempts",
                ]
            )
            .approvals(["security-lead", "architect"])
            .branch("feature/oauth2-auth")
            .log_path(Path("/var/log/auth-feature.log"))
            .build()
        )

        assert change.title == "Add user authentication"
        assert change.branch == "feature/oauth2-auth"
        assert change.log_path == Path("/var/log/auth-feature.log")
        assert len(change.shoulds) == 3
        assert len(change.musts) == 3
"}

1	from dataclasses import dataclass
2	from pathlib import Path
3	from textwrap import dedent
4	
5	
6	@dataclass(frozen=True)
7	class Change:
8	    title: str
9	    desc: str | None
10	    shoulds: list[str]
11	    musts: list[str]
12	    approvals: list[str]
13	    branch: str | None
14	    log_path: Path
15	
16	    def _format(self, title: str, steps: list[str]) -> str:
17	        if len(steps) == 0:
18	            return ""
19	        return title + "\n" + "\n".join(f"- {step}" for step in steps)
20	
21	    def full_description(self) -> str:
22	        return dedent(
23	            f"""
24	            --- {self.title} ---
25	            {self.desc}
26	
27	            {self._format("You SHOULD:", self.shoulds)}
28	
29	            {self._format("You MUST:", self.musts)}
30	            """
31	        ).strip()
32	
33	
34	class ChangeBuilder:
35	    def __init__(self, title: str):
36	        self._title = title
37	        self._desc = None
38	        self._shoulds: list[str] = []
39	        self._musts: list[str] = []
40	        self._approvals: list[str] = []
41	        self._branch = None
42	        self._log_path = None
43	
44	    def desc(self, desc: str) -> "ChangeBuilder":
45	        self._desc = dedent(desc).strip()
46	        return self
47	
48	    def shoulds(self, items: list[str]) -> "ChangeBuilder":
49	        self._shoulds = list(items)
50	        return self
51	
52	    def musts(self, items: list[str]) -> "ChangeBuilder":
53	        self._musts = list(items)
54	        return self
55	
56	    def approvals(self, items: list[str]) -> "ChangeBuilder":
57	        self._approvals = list(items)
58	        return self
59	
60	    def branch(self, branch: str) -> "ChangeBuilder":
61	        self._branch = branch
62	        return self
63	
64	    def log_path(self, log_path: Path) -> "ChangeBuilder":
65	        self._log_path = log_path
66	        return self
67	
68	    def build(self) -> Change:
69	        return Change(
70	            title=self._title,
71	            desc=self._desc,
72	            shoulds=self._shoulds,
73	            musts=self._musts,
74	            approvals=self._approvals,
75	            branch=self._branch,
76	            log_path=self._log_path,
77	        )

11	def apply_code_change(project: Project, change: Change, attempts: int = 20):
12	    logger.info(f"Starting code change for: {change.title}")
13	    coding_agent = CodingAgent(project, change)
14	    for attempt in range(attempts):
15	        response = coding_agent.start()
16	        logger.debug(f"[{attempt=}] Coding agent finished for: {change.title}")
17	
18	        must_answers = [PassFailAgent(project, must).answer() for must in change.musts]
19	        for result in must_answers:
20	            logger.debug(f"[{attempt=}] question={result.rule} answer={result.answer} reason={result.reason}")
21	
22	        failures = [result for result in must_answers if not result.answer]
23	        if len(failures) == 0:
24	            logger.info(f"[{attempt=}] No change.musts failed, done.")
25	            return response
26	
27	        coding_agent = CodingAgent(project, change)
28	        logger.info(f"[{attempt=}] Requesting coding agent fix {len(failures)} problem(s)")
29	
30	        message = ["This implementation is already in progress.", "The following code issues were identified:"]
31	        for failure in failures:
32	            message.append(f"<issue><rule>{failure.rule}</rule><reason>{failure.reason}</reason></issue>")
33	        message.append("")
34	        message.append("Please fix all issues.")
35	
36	        coding_agent.add_user_message("\n".join(message))
37	
38	    raise MaxAttemptsExceeded(f"Exhausted {attempts=}")

from pathlib import Path
from dataclasses import FrozenInstanceError
from tim.change import Change, ChangeBuilder


class TestChangeDataclass:
    def test_change_is_frozen(self):
        change = Change(
            title="Test Title",
            desc="Test description",
            shoulds=["should1"],
            musts=["must1"],
            approvals=["approver1"],
            branch="feature/test",
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.title = "Modified Title"

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.desc = "Modified description"

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.shoulds = ["modified"]

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.musts = ["modified"]

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.approvals = ["modified"]

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.branch = "modified"

        with pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError):
            change.log_path = Path("/modified/path")

    def test_change_field_access(self):
        change = Change(
            title="My Change",
            desc="A description",
            shoulds=["should do this"],
            musts=["must do that"],
            approvals=["alice", "bob"],
            branch="feature/my-feature",
            log_path=Path("/var/log/change.log"),
        )

        assert change.title == "My Change"
        assert change.desc == "A description"
        assert change.shoulds == ["should do this"]
        assert change.musts == ["must do that"]
        assert change.approvals == ["alice", "bob"]
        assert change.branch == "feature/my-feature"
        assert change.log_path == Path("/var/log/change.log")

        change = Change(

            shoulds=[],

class TestChangeFormat:
    def _make_change(self, **kwargs):
        return Change(
            title="Test",
            desc=None,
            shoulds=kwargs.get("shoulds", []),
            musts=kwargs.get("musts", []),
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

    def test_format_empty_steps(self):
        change = self._make_change()
        assert change._format("Test Title", []) == ""

    def test_format_single_step(self):
        change = self._make_change()
        result = change._format("Steps:", ["first step"])
        assert result == "Steps:\n- first step"

    def test_format_multiple_steps(self):
        change = self._make_change()
        result = change._format("Steps:", ["step 1", "step 2", "step 3"])
        assert result == "Steps:\n- step 1\n- step 2\n- step 3"

    def test_format_step_with_special_chars(self):
        change = self._make_change()
        result = change._format("Notes:", ["do 'this'", 'do "that"', "do & stuff"])
        assert result == "Notes:\n- do 'this'\n- do \"that\"\n- do & stuff"

    def test_format_step_with_newline_in_step(self):
        change = self._make_change()
        result = change._format("Steps:", ["step with\nembedded newline"])
        assert result == "Steps:\n- step with\nembedded newline"

        assert result == result.strip()

    def test_full_description_contains_title_separator(self):
        change = Change(
            title="Important Change",
            desc=None,
            shoulds=[],
            musts=[],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert "--- Important Change ---" in result

    def test_full_description_multiline_description(self):
        change = Change(
            title="Complex Change",
            desc="Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3",
            shoulds=["step 1"],
            musts=["step 2"],
            approvals=[],
            branch=None,
            log_path=Path("/tmp/log.txt"),
        )

        result = change.full_description()
        assert "Line 1" in result
        assert "Line 2" in result
        assert "Line 3" in result
        assert "step 1" in result
        assert "step 2" in result


class TestChangeBuilder:
    def test_basic_build_with_just_title(self):
        change = ChangeBuilder("My Change").build()

        assert change.title == "My Change"
        assert change.desc is None
        assert change.shoulds == []
        assert change.musts == []
        assert change.approvals == []
        assert change.branch is None
        assert change.log_path is None

    def test_building_with_all_fields(self):
        change = (
            ChangeBuilder("Complete Change")
            .desc("This is a complete change description")
            .shoulds(["should1", "should2"])
            .musts(["must1", "must2"])
            .approvals(["alice", "bob"])
            .branch("feature/complete")
            .log_path(Path("/var/log/complete.log"))
            .build()
        )

        assert change.title == "Complete Change"
        assert change.desc == "This is a complete change description"
        assert change.shoulds == ["should1", "should2"]
        assert change.musts == ["must1", "must2"]
        assert change.approvals == ["alice", "bob"]
        assert change.branch == "feature/complete"
        assert change.log_path == Path("/var/log/complete.log")

    def test_chaining_methods(self):
        builder = ChangeBuilder("Chained")
        assert builder.desc("desc").shoulds(["s1"]) is not None
        assert builder.musts(["m1"]).approvals(["a1"]) is not None
        assert builder.branch("main").log_path(Path("/tmp")) is not None

        change = builder.build()
        assert change.title == "Chained"
        assert change.desc == "desc"
        assert change.shoulds == ["s1"]
        assert change.musts == ["m1"]
        assert change.approvals == ["a1"]
        assert change.branch == "main"
        assert change.log_path == Path("/tmp")

    def test_default_values_for_optional_fields(self):
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").build()

        assert change.desc is None
        assert change.shoulds == []
        assert change.musts == []
        assert change.approvals == []
        assert change.branch is None
        assert change.log_path is None

    def test_desc_method_with_dedent(self):
        change = (
            ChangeBuilder("Test")
            .desc(
                """
                This is a multi-line description.
                It has leading indentation.
                """
            )
            .build()
        )

        assert change.desc == "This is a multi-line description.\nIt has leading indentation."

    def test_desc_method_strips_whitespace(self):
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").desc("   \n  leading and trailing spaces  \n  ").build()

        assert change.desc == "leading and trailing spaces"

    def test_shoulds_with_empty_list(self):
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").shoulds([]).build()
        assert change.shoulds == []

    def test_shoulds_with_items(self):
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").shoulds(["item1", "item2"]).build()
        assert change.shoulds == ["item1", "item2"]

    def test_shoulds_copies_list(self):
        items = ["item1"]
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").shoulds(items).build()

        items.append("item2")
        assert change.shoulds == ["item1"]

    def test_musts_with_empty_list(self):
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").musts([]).build()
        assert change.musts == []

    def test_musts_with_items(self):
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").musts(["item1", "item2"]).build()
        assert change.musts == ["item1", "item2"]

    def test_musts_copies_list(self):
        items = ["item1"]
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").musts(items).build()

        items.append("item2")
        assert change.musts == ["item1"]

    def test_approvals_with_empty_list(self):
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").approvals([]).build()
        assert change.approvals == []

    def test_approvals_with_items(self):
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").approvals(["alice", "bob"]).build()
        assert change.approvals == ["alice", "bob"]

    def test_approvals_copies_list(self):
        items = ["alice"]
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").approvals(items).build()

        items.append("bob")
        assert change.approvals == ["alice"]

    def test_branch_setting_branch_name(self):
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").branch("feature/test-branch").build()
        assert change.branch == "feature/test-branch"

    def test_branch_default_is_none(self):
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").build()
        assert change.branch is None

    def test_log_path_setting_log_path(self):
        path = Path("/var/log/my-change.log")
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").log_path(path).build()
        assert change.log_path == path

    def test_log_path_default_is_none(self):
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").build()
        assert change.log_path is None

    def test_builder_returns_change_instance(self):
        change = ChangeBuilder("Test").build()
        assert isinstance(change, Change)

    def test_multiple_builds_independent(self):
        change1 = ChangeBuilder("First").desc("First desc").build()
        change2 = ChangeBuilder("Second").desc("Second desc").build()

        assert change1.title == "First"
        assert change2.title == "Second"
        assert change1.desc == "First desc"
        assert change2.desc == "Second desc"

    def test_desc_with_complex_indentation(self):
        change = (
            ChangeBuilder("Test")
            .desc(
                """
                    This has 4 spaces of indentation.
                        And this has 8 spaces of indentation.
                    Back to 4 spaces.
                """
            )
            .build()
        )

        assert "This has 4 spaces of indentation." in change.desc
        assert "And this has 8 spaces of indentation." in change.desc
        assert "Back to 4 spaces." in change.desc

    def test_full_builder_workflow(self):
        change = (
            ChangeBuilder("Add user authentication")
            .desc(
                """
                Implement OAuth2 authentication for the API.
                This will allow third-party applications to
                access the API securely.
                """
            )
            .shoulds(
                [
                    "Use PKCE for public clients",
                    "Refresh tokens after expiration",
                    "Cache access tokens efficiently",
                ]
            )
            .musts(
                [
                    "Pass all security audit checks",
                    "Support RFC 6749 compliance",
                    "Log all authentication attempts",
                ]
            )
            .approvals(["security-lead", "architect"])
            .branch("feature/oauth2-auth")
            .log_path(Path("/var/log/auth-feature.log"))
            .build()
        )

        assert change.title == "Add user authentication"
        assert change.branch == "feature/oauth2-auth"
        assert change.log_path == Path("/var/log/auth-feature.log")
        assert len(change.shoulds) == 3
        assert len(change.musts) == 3
